message.content:
Try HARD to REFUTE this lever. Default refuted=true if uncertain. Read /home/polaris/polaris_project/SOTA_BRIEF_V2.md, the artifacts at /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board, and the grader source if needed.

LEVER: Dedup + cross-reference + kill the overflow bin (L7)
RUBRIC CRITERION CLAIMED: P1: Clarity and Synthesis in Presenting Sourced Information :: ...Checks for appropriate information density and avoidance of redundancy (readability w=0.15); Breadth of Labor Market Restructuring Dimensions Covered (comprehensiveness w=0.25, damaged by assembly-by-section repetition) [readability + comprehensiveness]
CHANGE: Deterministic near-duplicate detector (same [n] set + >0.8 content-token overlap: measured dupes — Eloundou 1.8%/46% x2, Noy-Zhang 2x/1.6x x2, 5.4% adoption x3): keep the instance in the section where it does analytical work, replace others with a cross-reference clause ('as discussed in Section 3.2'). Dissolve '## Additional Corroborated Findings' by routing its facts into thematic sections via the multi_section_outline.json sidecar; section-title denylist ('Additional', 'Corroborated', 'Miscellaneous').
CLAIMED: +0.003 to +0.008 overall
ADMITTED FAILURE MODE: Cross-references into a reflowed document need stable section numbers — must run after L2 freezes structure; routing overflow facts by outline similarity can misfile them (a misfiled fact is a coherence hit, not a faithfulness hit — acceptable, revertable)
FAITHFULNESS CONTRACT: REVISED CONTRACT — from hedged gloss to contained adjudication. The old contract's guarantee ('worst case is a hedged, uncited, capped, contradiction-screened relational gloss') aimed at the wrong genre: the rubric pays for ADJUDICATION ('areas of consensus, debate, or uncertainty... rather than merely cataloging effects'), and mandatory hedge lexemes plus an 8% budget structurally forbid it. The revision keeps every rule that prevents fabrication and deletes only the rules that enforce timidity. CLASS F (unchanged): span-verified, byte-identical; the multiset validator now permits DELETIONS from an explicit audited drop-list (L1 debris, L5 non-journal filter, L7 dedup) — deletion cannot fabricate; mutation remains impossible. CLASS F+A (new): a FACT sentence with attribution injected, where every injected author/venue/year string must be a byte-substring of the bibliography.json or Crossref record keyed by that sentence's own [n]; validator-enforced, so a fabricated attribution cannot pass a string check. CLASS S (unchanged) plus claim-first heading check (finite verb; still no digit, no citation, no new proper noun). CLASS I (redesigned): a sentence is legal iff (1) NO digit, %, unit, or spelled-out quantity — unchanged, absolute; (2) NO citation marker, NO quotation mark — unchanged; (3) NO new proper noun, NO un-named attribution — unchanged; (4-REVISED) it instantiates exactly one TYPED MOVE with a visible label — **Interpretation**, **Mechanism link**, **Implication**, **Reconciliation**, or [Analytical synthesis] — the label IS the containment: it tells the judge (and any reader) where evidence ends and argument begins, which is cellcog's exact mechanism for unhedged original claims without fabrication. Hedge lexemes are permitted, never required. Adjudicative predicates ('depends on', 'is better read as', 'situates X as', 'reconciles with... if') are legal. Still banned: universals, 'will'/forecasts, and bare EMPIRICAL-OUTCOME assertions (a study-referent subject + outcome verb + direction: 'employment fell') — an adjudication rules on how findings RELATE; it may not mint a new finding; (5) premise adjacency unchanged: >=2 FACT sentences, >=2 distinct sources, deictic pointer, content-stem anchor — plus a new VACUITY check: a Reconciliation must share content stems with BOTH premises; (6-REVISED) budget I_BUDGET_FRAC 0.08 -> 0.35, <=1 labeled move per paragraph, plus one dedicated Critical Synthesis section of reconciliation triplets; (7) fail-closed contradiction screen unchanged: CONTRADICTED, judge error, or sentinel => dropped, never kept. GUARANTEE (revised): the worst content that can ship is a labeled, digit-free, entity-free, premise-anchored, contradiction-screened relational ruling over already-verified facts. It can be wrong in EMPHASIS — which is the licensed, labeled genre of a literature review — but it cannot state a fact, number, quote, or attribution that is not already verified, because those are deterministic string checks, not model discipline. No fabricated fact/number/entity ships, ever; any validator breach reverts the paragraph, any structural breach reverts the document.

ATTACK THROUGH THIS LENS:
FABRICATION: does this lever create ANY path for an ungrounded fact, number, entity or attribution to reach the page? The faithfulness moat is not for sale — a lever that leaks is unshippable at ANY score. Trace the concrete path.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# BRIEF V2 — EVERYTHING WE NOW KNOW. Design the plan that takes POLARIS to the top of the board.
2	# (Every number here is MEASURED from primary artifacts on disk or read from the grader's own source code.)
3	
4	## 1. THE BOARD (task 72, OUR evaluator = GPT-5.5, per-task scores from the benchmark's own raw_results)
5	| system | OVERALL | compreh | INSIGHT | instr-follow | readability | words | H3 | med-para | bullets |
6	|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
7	| **bodhi** (task-72 winner) | **0.5441** | 0.5441 | 0.5457 | 0.5559 | 0.5199 | **4,361** | 40 | 59w | 14 |
8	| lunon | 0.5406 | 0.5437 | 0.5594 | 0.5323 | 0.5037 | 11,476 | 50 | 71w | 11 |
9	| WhaleCloud | 0.5396 | 0.5417 | 0.5429 | 0.5393 | 0.5284 | 7,892 | 33 | 63w | 26 |
10	| dalpha | 0.5252 | 0.5213 | 0.5200 | 0.5350 | 0.5284 | 4,039 | 0 | 38w | 0 |
11	| sourcery | 0.5205 | 0.5211 | 0.5397 | 0.5033 | 0.5035 | 9,849 | 38 | 66w | 106 |
12	| **gemini-2.5-pro-DR = THE REFERENCE** | 0.5102 | 0.5111 | 0.5035 | 0.5187 | 0.5100 | 7,907 | 0 | 131w | 61 |
13	| openai-DR | 0.4743 | 0.4789 | 0.4475 | 0.4982 | 0.4859 | 8,513 | 0 | 170w | 13 |
14	| **POLARIS (rank10)** | **0.4382** | 0.4549 | 0.4238 | 0.4409 | **0.3774** | 7,742 | **0** | **677w** | 0 |
15	| grok | 0.4316 | 0.4292 | 0.3873 | 0.4670 | 0.4706 | 1,060 | 0 | 72w | 0 |
16	| perplexity | 0.4241 | 0.4228 | 0.4015 | 0.4391 | 0.4507 | 1,510 | 0 | 58w | 0 |
17	(cellcog-max = 0.5578 corpus-wide #1; it publishes no per-task breakdown. Its task-72 artifact: 16,334w, 31 H3, 75w paras, 0 bullets.)
18	**"ADORE 52.65" IS FICTION** — absent from the backing data of both boards. Do not plan against it.
19	
20	## 2. THE GAP, DECOMPOSED (us -> bodhi)
21	| dim | gap | weight | overall |
22	|---|---|---|---|
23	| INSIGHT | +0.122 | 0.32 | **+0.039** |
24	| instruction-following | +0.115 | 0.25 | +0.029 |
25	| comprehensiveness | +0.089 | 0.29 | +0.026 |
26	| readability | **+0.143** | 0.14 | +0.020 |
27	| | | | **= +0.114 -> 0.552** |
28	**OUR READABILITY (0.3774) IS THE WORST SCORE ON THE ENTIRE BOARD** — below grok and perplexity, the two systems we beat.
29	
30	## 3. HOW THE GRADER WORKS (read from its source, not inferred)
31	- **ONE LLM call scores BOTH reports SIDE BY SIDE.** Ours = article_1, reference = article_2, always. (race.py:99-104)
32	- **0-10 per criterion**, ~25 criteria. Bands: 4-6 "Average", **6-8 "Good"**, **8-10 "Excellent"**.
33	  We are being read at ~6.6; the reference reads ~9.0. **To beat parity a criterion must cross from "Good" into "Excellent". There is no credit for being a better 7.**
34	- **Overall = ours/(ours+reference)** — COMPRESSIVE. +1.0 raw point on EVERY criterion = only **+0.036 overall**.
35	- **THE JUDGE WRITES A COMPARATIVE ANALYSIS BEFORE IT SCORES** (forced chain-of-thought, prompt lines 47-75), and
36	  **ALL ~25 CRITERIA ACROSS ALL 4 DIMENSIONS ARE SCORED IN ONE CALL, ONE CONTEXT.** Nothing firewalls the dimensions.
37	  **=> CROSS-DIMENSION BLEED IS REAL AND UNINSTRUCTED.** The narrative it writes about our 677-word walls is still in
38	  context when it scores our insight. Our readability disaster is taxing the other 86% of the score.
39	- **Dimension weights (100-task mean): insight 0.352 | comprehensiveness 0.292 | instruction-following 0.215 | readability 0.141.**
40	  Insight + comprehensiveness = **64% of the score**.
41	- **LENGTH IS NEVER MENTIONED IN THE SCORING PROMPT.** No criterion rewards it. Padding is penalised at only ~0.7% of
42	  overall, while substance-bearing expansion pays into 64%. => "longer but DENSER" wins; "longer and thinner" is pointless.
43	- **EXECUTIVE SUMMARIES ARE NOT REWARDED.** Proof: the reference ships an EMPTY PLACEHOLDER exec summary
44	  ("this section will be written after the report body is complete") and still beats every system on every task.
45	- **HEDGING IS NOT REWARDED.** No criterion anywhere pays for softness or uncertainty-as-stance. What IS rewarded (51
46	  criteria, inside the 64%-weight dimensions) is **CRITICAL SCRUTINY**: "Critical Evaluation of Evidence and Synthesis of
47	  Competing Theories", "Critical Scrutiny of Limitations". **The judge does not want us to sound uncertain. It wants us to ADJUDICATE.**
48	- INSIGHT rubric, verbatim: "deep analysis and original insights rather than simply repeating known information";
49	  "clear logical reasoning and effectively explains causal relationships behind phenomena"; "identifies key issues ...
50	  provides insightful solutions"; "forward-looking thinking, can anticipate trends".
51	- COMPREHENSIVENESS rubric: breadth AND depth AND **evidence** AND "multiple perspectives and balance" — NOT "more sections".
52	- Structure: "Overall Article Structure and Logical Flow" is the largest readability criterion and explicitly names
53	  "distinct heading levels" / "effective headings and subheadings". Tables/charts: max ~2.8% of overall. Worth having, not worth optimising hard.
54	- **RACE STRIPS EVERY CITATION MARKER AND THE ENTIRE BIBLIOGRAPHY BEFORE JUDGING** (utils/clean_article.py, an LLM
55	  cleaner). VERIFIED: our 9,300 words -> 7,692 read; 345 [n] markers -> 0; 105-entry bibliography -> DELETED.
56	  **=> ALL bibliography/citation-quality work scores ZERO. Source quality can ONLY be signalled by NAMING VENUES/AUTHORS
57	  IN THE RUNNING PROSE.** (The 0.5102 reference ships ZERO inline citations.)
58	
59	## 4. WHAT THE WINNERS DO (measured from their task-72 artifacts)
60	- **5 of the 6 systems above the reference have 31-50 H3 subsections. Every system at/below the reference has ZERO.**
61	  (dalpha is the lone exception: 0 H3, but 38-word paragraphs.) **We have 0 H3 and 677-word paragraphs.**
62	- Winners' median paragraph: **59-75 words**. Reference: 131. OpenAI: 170. **Us: 677 — a different regime entirely.**
63	- **LENGTH IS DEAD AS A LEVER: bodhi WINS TASK 72 ON 4,361 WORDS — 44% SHORTER THAN OURS.** dalpha scores 0.5252 on 4,039.
64	- Bullets are NOT the lever (sourcery ships 106 and beats the reference; cellcog ships 0). Tables are NOT the lever (0-5).
65	- cellcog's synthesis pattern (measured in its prose): grounded finding A (named study + number) -> grounded finding B
66	  (a CONFLICTING named study) -> a reconciliation sentence carrying NO new facts -> enumerated candidate explanations ->
67	  an explicit statement of what the evidence CANNOT yet resolve. Plus a dedicated "Critical Synthesis" section
68	  (convergence / outstanding tensions / limitations / future research).
69	- Winners ADAPT SHAPE TO GENRE (bullet-density varies 3-10x between applied and scholarly tasks); losers apply one template.
70	
71	## 5. WHAT POLARIS IS (the constraint you must design around)
72	- An extraction-grounded FACT CONVEYOR. `verify_sentence_provenance` (provenance_generator.py:2098) admits a sentence
73	  only if `len(failures)==0`; the entailment judge's NEUTRAL clause (entailment_judge.py:588) kills any sentence that
74	  "introduces a fact, entity, **MECHANISM** ... NOT present in the SPAN".
75	- **A cross-source inference is, BY DEFINITION, a mechanism not present in either span. Our own gate deletes exactly the
76	  sentence type that earns INSIGHT (0.32 — the heaviest weight, and our worst dimension).**
77	- Boundary, precisely: a CONJUNCTION of two sources ("X found 2.1% [a] while Y found 0.4% [b]") PASSES. Add one
78	  interpretive clause ("a gap that may reflect slower adoption") and it is KILLED.
79	- **FAITHFULNESS IS THE MOAT AND IS NOT FOR SALE.** No fabricated fact, number, quote or attribution may ever ship. Any
80	  design that can leak one is unshippable, whatever it scores. We have a working deterministic contract
81	  (scripts/reflow_report.py): FACT (span-verified, byte-identical) / STRUCTURE (assembled from sidecar data) /
82	  INTERPRETATION (no digits, no new proper nouns, no citations, must sit beside >=2 facts from >=2 sources,
83	  contradiction-screened fail-closed). **NOTE: it was built around HEDGING — and the rubric does not reward hedging.
84	  It rewards ADJUDICATION. That contract needs rethinking, not just retuning.**
85	
86	## 6. MEASUREMENT (fixed tonight — use it, never score n=1 again)
87	- Judge noise: **SD = 0.0074** (byte-identical artifact scored 6x). Baseline is tighter (SD 0.0020).
88	- k=5 paired => SE(diff) = 0.0047 => **smallest resolvable effect = +0.0094 at 2 sigma.**
89	- Generation noise (re-composing the same config) is LARGER (~0.016) and applies ONLY to arms needing a new compose.
90	  A report->report transform of a BANKED artifact has ZERO generation noise — cheap AND sensitive. Prefer it.
91	- Our own history: baseline 0.4062 -> rank10 0.4382 = **+0.032, z=10** (real). The depth levers DID work, modestly.
92	  But every subsequent lever (padding removal, source firewall) scored FLAT. And ALL of it was aimed at proxies.
93	
94	## 7. YOUR TASK
95	Design the plan that takes POLARIS from **0.4382** to **the top of this board (0.54-0.56)**.
96	- Ground every lever in the rubric text or a measured artifact. NO PROXIES — we lost a night to them.
97	- State, for each lever: the criterion it moves, the expected points, how it FAILS, and the cheap test that proves the
98	  mechanism fired BEFORE we pay for a 65-minute compose.
99	- Respect the faithfulness moat absolutely. If a lever needs ungrounded prose, say so explicitly and justify the exact
100	  containment.
101	- Be honest about the ceiling. If your plan does not reach 0.54, SAY SO and name the residual.
102	- Where you are UNCERTAIN, SEARCH (papers, GitHub, the artifacts on disk at
103	  /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/). Do not guess.
104

content.text: Let me examine the actual POLARIS artifact and the faithfulness contract code.

282	def validate_interpretation(sent: str, premises: list[str]) -> tuple[bool, str]:
283	    """Deterministic gate for a CLASS I sentence. No LLM.
284	
285	    Returns (ok, reasons). ALL violated rules are reported, not just the first -- an attack that
286	    trips three rules should say so, otherwise a rule can silently rot behind an earlier one (the
287	    old code reported 'no_epistemic_frame' for the Goldman-Sachs attack and the proper-noun rule
288	    was never actually exercised by the suite).
289	    """
290	    s = sent.strip()
291	    if not s:
292	        return False, "empty"
293	    low = s.lower()
294	    bad: list[str] = []
295	
296	    # (1) no checkable quantity -- digits, units, or the same number spelled out in words
297	    if DIGIT_RE.search(s):
298	        bad.append("contains_digit")
299	    if UNIT_RE.search(s):
300	        bad.append("contains_unit")
301	    h = _hits(NUMBER_WORDS_BANNED, low)
302	    if h:
303	        bad.append(f"spelled_out_quantity:{h}")
304	
305	    # (2) no citation marker (never masquerade as verified), no quotation (attributable speech)
306	    if CITE_RE.search(s):
307	        bad.append("carries_citation_marker")
308	    if QUOTE_RE.search(s):
309	        bad.append("contains_quotation")
310	
311	    # (4) epistemic frame required; certainty, forecasts, result assertions and negated findings banned
312	    if not any(re.search(rf"\b{re.escape(e)}\b", low) for e in EPISTEMIC):
313	        bad.append("no_epistemic_frame")
314	    h = _hits(CERTAINTY_BANNED, low)
315	    if h:
316	        bad.append(f"certainty_or_overclaim:{h}")
317	    h = _hits(RESULT_VERBS_BANNED, low)
318	    if h:
319	        bad.append(f"bare_result_assertion:{h}")
320	    h = _hits(NEGATED_FINDING_BANNED, low)
321	    if h:
322	        bad.append(f"negated_finding:{h}")
323	
324	    # (3) attribution smuggled in without a proper noun
325	    h = _hits(ATTRIBUTION_BANNED, low)
326	    if h:
327	        bad.append(f"unnamed_attribution:{h}")
328	
329	    # (5) must interpret >= 2 facts from >= 2 distinct sources
330	    facts = [p for p in premises if is_fact_sentence(p)]
331	    srcs: set[str] = set()
332	    for p in facts:
333	        srcs |= cited_ids(p)
334	    if len(facts) < 2 or len(srcs) < 2:
335	        bad.append(f"insufficient_premises(facts={len(facts)},sources={len(srcs)})")
336	
337	    premise_blob = " ".join(premises)
338	
339	    # (3) no new proper noun: every capitalised token must already appear in the premises
340	    premise_caps = set(CAP_TOKEN_RE.findall(premise_blob))
341	    for t in CAP_TOKEN_RE.findall(s):
342	        if t in CONCEPT_WHITELIST or t in premise_caps:
343	            continue
344	        # a sentence-initial capital of a word that is otherwise lowercase in the premises is fine
345	        if s.startswith(t) and re.search(rf"\b{re.escape(t.lower())}\b", premise_blob.lower()):
346	            continue
347	        bad.append(f"new_proper_noun:{t}")
348	        break
349	
350	    # (5) it must POINT AT these premises and share vocabulary with them
351	    if not any(d in low for d in DEICTIC):
352	        bad.append("no_deictic_pointer")
353	    if len(content_stems(low) & content_stems(premise_blob)) < ANCHOR_MIN:
354	        bad.append("insufficient_premise_anchor")
355	
356	    if bad:
357	        return False, ",".join(bad)
358	    return True, ""
359	
360	
361	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- class-S heading gate
362	
363	def validate_heading(heading_text: str, premises: list[str]) -> tuple[bool, str]:
364	    """`###` headings are the only LLM-authored class-S text -- gate them like class-I lite.
365	
366	    A heading cannot hedge, so no epistemic frame is required; but it may not carry a number, a
367	    citation, or a name that is not already in the section's facts.
368	    """
369	    h = heading_text.strip().lstrip("#").strip()
370	    if not h:
371	        return False, "empty"
372	    bad: list[str] = []
373	    if DIGIT_RE.search(h):
374	        bad.append("contains_digit")
375	    if UNIT_RE.search(h):
376	        bad.append("contains_unit")
377	    if CITE_RE.search(h):
378	        bad.append("carries_citation_marker")
379	    if len(h.split()) > HEADING_MAX_WORDS:
380	        bad.append(f"too_long({len(h.split())}w)")
381	    hit = _hits(CERTAINTY_BANNED, h.lower())
382	    if hit:
383	        bad.append(f"certainty_or_overclaim:{hit}")
384	    blob = " ".join(premises)
385	    caps = set(CAP_TOKEN_RE.findall(blob))
386	    for t in CAP_TOKEN_RE.findall(h):
387	        if t in CONCEPT_WHITELIST or t in caps:
388	            continue
389	        if h.startswith(t) and re.search(rf"\b{re.escape(t.lower())}\b", blob.lower()):
390	            continue
391	        bad.append(f"new_proper_noun:{t}")
392	        break
393	    return (not bad), ",".join(bad)
394	
395	
396	def gate_headings(section_md: str, premises: list[str]) -> tuple[str, int]:
397	    """DELETE any `###` heading that fails the gate. Deleting a class-S line cannot touch prose."""
398	    out: list[str] = []
399	    dropped = 0
400	    for line in section_md.split("\n"):
401	        if re.match(r"^\s*###+\s+\S", line):
402	            ok, why = validate_heading(line, premises)
403	            if not ok:
404	                print(f"    [heading REJECTED] {why}: {line.strip()[:70]!r}")
405	                dropped += 1
406	                continue
407	        out.append(line)
408	    return "\n".join(out), dropped
409	
410	
411	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- structural validator
412	
413	def validate_reflow(
414	    src_body: str, out_body: str, approved_i: list[str] | None = None
415	) -> tuple[bool, list[str]]:
416	    """The load-bearing check. Any failure => caller reverts to source.
417	
418	    (a) the FACT-sentence multiset is IDENTICAL (regroup/reorder only -- never invented, mutated,
419	        dropped);
420	    (b) every NON-fact sentence shipped is byte-identical to a source sentence or is an APPROVED
421	        class-I sentence -- so no other new prose can exist anywhere in the document;
422	    (c) prose did not grow beyond source + approved class-I words. Word counts are taken on
423	        strip_md'd prose: markdown chrome (headings, bullet markers, table pipes) is class S and is
424	        deliberately outside the budget, otherwise "regroup into bullets" is impossible by
425	        construction.
426	    """
427	    errs: list[str] = []
428	    approved = [re.sub(r"\s+", " ", s).strip() for s in (approved_i or [])]
429	
430	    src_prose = strip_md(src_body)
431	    out_prose = strip_md(out_body)
432	    src_sents = split_sentences(src_prose)
433	    out_sents = split_sentences(out_prose)
434	
435	    # TRUE multiset diff (Counter, not `in`): a DUPLICATED fact sentence is present in both lists,
436	    # so a membership test reports neither a loss nor an addition and the duplication slips through
437	    # on to the word-count check alone. Counter subtraction catches all three: loss, invention, dup.
438	    src_facts = Counter(s for s in src_sents if is_fact_sentence(s))
439	    out_facts = Counter(s for s in out_sents if is_fact_sentence(s))
440	    if src_facts != out_facts:
441	        missing = list((src_facts - out_facts).elements())
442	        added = list((out_facts - src_facts).elements())
443	        if missing:
444	            errs.append(f"FACT_SENTENCE_LOST({len(missing)}): {missing[0][:90]!r}")
445	        if added:
446	            dup = [s for s in added if s in src_facts]
447	            tag = "FACT_SENTENCE_DUPLICATED" if dup else "FACT_SENTENCE_INVENTED_OR_MUTATED"
448	            errs.append(f"{tag}({len(added)}): {added[0][:90]!r}")
449	
450	    # (b) no unapproved new prose
451	    src_nonfact = {s for s in src_sents if not is_fact_sentence(s)}
452	    for s in out_sents:
453	        if is_fact_sentence(s) or s in src_nonfact:
454	            continue
455	        if any(s == a or a in s for a in approved):
456	            continue
457	        errs.append(f"UNAPPROVED_NEW_PROSE: {s[:90]!r}")
458	        break
459	
460	    # (c) prose budget
461	    budget = len(src_prose.split()) + sum(len(a.split()) for a in approved)
462	    if len(out_prose.split()) > budget:
463	        errs.append(
464	            f"BODY_GREW: {len(src_prose.split())}(+{budget - len(src_prose.split())} approved) "
465	            f"-> {len(out_prose.split())} words"
466	        )
467	    return (not errs), errs
468	
469	
470	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- W4: confession purge
471

100	CAP_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\b([A-Z][A-Za-z&.\-']{1,})\b")
101	
102	EPISTEMIC = (
103	    "may", "might", "could", "suggests", "suggest", "appears", "seems", "consistent with",
104	    "plausibly", "likely reflects", "one reading", "this pattern", "these findings", "tension",
105	    "not necessarily", "rather than", "whereas", "in contrast", "taken together", "indicate",
106	)
107	# (5) the sentence must POINT AT its premises, not free-float above them.
108	DEICTIC = (
109	    "these findings", "this pattern", "taken together", "the divergence", "this divergence",
110	    "the contrast", "this contrast", "these results", "the tension", "this tension",
111	    "these studies", "the two literatures", "both literatures", "this disagreement",
112	    "the gap between", "these estimates", "read together", "seen together",
113	)
114	
115	# (4) certainty / universals / forecasts. Word-boundary matched -- "never" must not fire inside
116	# "nevertheless", and "ten" must not fire inside "tension".
117	CERTAINTY_BANNED = (
118	    r"\ball\b", r"\bevery\b", r"\balways\b", r"\bnever\b", r"\bcertainly\b", r"\bcertain\b",
119	    r"\bproves?\b", r"\bproven\b", r"\bdefinitively\b", r"\bguarantees?\b", r"\binevitab\w*\b",
120	    r"\bwill\b", r"\bwould replace\b", r"\bshall\b", r"\bundoubtedly\b", r"\bclearly demonstrates?\b",
121	    r"\bwithout question\b", r"\bno doubt\b",
122	)
123	# (4) a bare result assertion is a FACT claim, not an interpretation. An interpretation RELATES
124	# findings ("may reflect", "is consistent with"); it does not re-assert measured outcomes.
125	RESULT_VERBS_BANNED = (
126	    r"\b(rose|rises|risen|fell|fall[s]?|fallen|increased|increases|decreased|decreases|declined|"
127	    r"declines|grew|grows|dropped|drops|surged|surges|plummeted|doubled|tripled|halved|quadrupled|"
128	    r"outperformed|outperforms|exceeded|exceeds|gained|lost|reached|totalled|totaled|amounted)\b",
129	)
130	# (4) polarity inversion with no digits anywhere: "no gain was observed", "the effect disappeared".
131	NEGATED_FINDING_BANNED = (
132	    r"\bno\s+(gain|gains|increase|increases|decrease|decreases|change|changes|effect|effects|impact|"
133	    r"impacts|difference|differences|evidence|correlation|association|displacement|growth|improvement)\b",
134	    r"\bno\s+\w+\s+(was|were|is|are)\s+(observed|found|detected|reported|seen|measured|recorded)\b",
135	    r"\b(nothing|none of|zero)\b",
136	    r"\b(was|were|is|are)\s+not\s+(observed|found|detected|reported|seen|significant)\b",
137	    r"\bfailed to\b", r"\bdisappear(ed|s)?\b", r"\bvanish(ed|es)?\b",
138	    r"\bdid not\s+(rise|fall|increase|decrease|change|grow|improve|reduce|appear)\b",
139	)
140	# (3) attribution without a proper noun -- "according to analysts", "one study finds".
141	ATTRIBUTION_BANNED = (
142	    r"\baccording to\b", r"\banalysts?\b", r"\bexperts?\b", r"\beconomists?\b", r"\bresearchers?\b",
143	    r"\bthe authors?\b", r"\bone study\b", r"\ba (recent |new )?(report|study|survey|paper|analysis)\b",
144	    r"\b(studies|reports|surveys|papers)\s+(show|shows|find|finds|report|reports|conclude|concludes)\b",
145	    r"\bresearch (shows|finds|suggests|indicates)\b", r"\bevidence from\b", r"\bcommentators?\b",
146	)
147	# (1) spelled-out quantities. A checkable number in words is still a checkable number.
148	NUMBER_WORDS_BANNED = (
149	    r"\b(zero|one|two|three|four|five|six|seven|eight|nine|ten|eleven|twelve|thirteen|fourteen|"
150	    r"fifteen|sixteen|seventeen|eighteen|nineteen|twenty|thirty|forty|fifty|sixty|seventy|eighty|"
151	    r"ninety|hundred|thousand|million|billion|trillion|dozen)\b",
152	    r"\b(half|halves|third|thirds|quarter|quarters|fifth|fifths|tenth|tenths|percent|percentage|"
153	    r"percentages|pp|basis points?)\b",
154	    r"\b(twice|thrice|double|doubles|doubling|triple|triples|tripling|quadruple|quadrupling|"
155	    r"halving|fold|twofold|threefold|fourfold|fivefold|tenfold|hundredfold)\b",
156	    r"\border(s)? of magnitude\b",
157	    r"\b(majority|minority|most of|few of|several of)\b",
158	)
159	UNIT_RE = re.compile(r"%|\b(percent|percentage points?|pp|bps|usd|eur|gbp)\b", re.I)
160	
161	# Concepts named in the task prompt itself -- legal discourse vocabulary, not new entities.
162	# NOTHING in here is or can become an entity name; that is the invariant this set must keep.
163	CONCEPT_WHITELIST = {
164	    "AI", "Artificial", "Intelligence", "Fourth", "Industrial", "Revolution", "4IR", "The", "This",
165	    "These", "Those", "Taken", "While", "Whereas", "Although", "Because", "Such", "One", "Both",
166	    "Together", "In", "At", "By", "For", "A", "An", "It", "They", "Their", "Its", "However",
167	    "Yet", "Still", "Across", "Within", "Where", "When", "If", "As", "That", "Neither", "Either",
168	    "Industry", "Labor", "Labour", "Market", "Work", "Workers", "Jobs", "Skills", "Wages",
169	    "Productivity", "Automation", "Employment", "Displacement", "Augmentation", "Adoption",
170	    # heading openers (class-S only): generic academic connectives, never entity names
171	    "Reconciling", "Evidence", "Mechanisms", "Mechanism", "Scope", "Limits", "Limitations",
172	    "Divergence", "Convergence", "Findings", "Effects", "Task", "Aggregate", "Experimental",
173	    "Administrative", "Firm", "Firms", "Sectoral", "Sector", "Distributional", "Timing",
174	    "Measurement", "Interpretation", "Reading", "Beyond", "From", "What", "Why", "How",
175	}
176	
177	STOPWORDS = {
178	    "about", "above", "after", "again", "against", "along", "already", "also", "although", "among",
179	    "appears", "because", "been", "before", "being", "below", "between", "beyond", "both", "could",
180	    "does", "each", "even", "ever", "from", "have", "here", "into", "itself", "just", "like",
181	    "likely", "may", "might", "more", "most", "much", "must", "only", "other", "over", "rather",
182	    "same", "seems", "should", "since", "some", "such", "taken", "than", "that", "their", "them",
183	    "then", "there", "these", "they", "this", "those", "through", "thus", "together", "under",
184	    "until", "very", "were", "what", "when", "where", "which", "while", "with", "within",
185	    "without", "would", "your", "consistent", "suggests", "suggest", "indicate", "whereas",
186	}
187	
188	ANCHOR_MIN = 1          # see the ONE HONEST CONCESSION note in the module docstring
189	I_BUDGET_FRAC = 0.08    # rule (6): class-I <= 8% of body sentences
190	HEADING_MAX_WORDS = 12
191	
192	# Sentence terminator: [.!?] plus any trailing quotes/brackets AND any trailing [n] citation
193	# markers (the corpus writes "novices.[1]" -- citation AFTER the period), followed by whitespace
194	# and a new sentence opener. The terminator is CAPTURED so splitting never eats a citation.
195	SENT_BOUNDARY_RE = re.compile(
196	    r"([.!?]+[\"'’”\)\]]*(?:\s*\[\d+\])*)\s+(?=[A-Z\[\(\"“])"
197	)

232	def is_fact_sentence(s: str) -> bool:
233	    """FACT = carries a citation marker or a number. Only these may assert anything checkable."""
234	    return bool(CITE_RE.search(s)) or bool(DIGIT_RE.search(s))
235

237	def cited_ids(s: str) -> set[str]:
238	    return set(CITE_RE.findall(s))

244	    t = re.sub(r"^\s*\|.*$", "", t, flags=re.M)             # tables   (class S)

content.text:
REFUTED. Traced a concrete fabrication path in the source.

**The mechanical blocker (verified in `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/reflow_report.py`):**

```python
def is_fact_sentence(s: str) -> bool:   # line 232-234
    return bool(CITE_RE.search(s)) or bool(DIGIT_RE.search(s))   # DIGIT_RE = r"\d"
```

`is_fact_sentence` is the routing function on which the *entire* moat rests: any digit-bearing sentence is a FACT, and `validate_reflow` (lines 438-448) demands `Counter(src_facts) == Counter(out_facts)` — byte-identical multiset.

L7's cross-reference clause is `"as discussed in Section 3.2"`. **It contains digits.** Any sentence carrying it is classified as a FACT sentence, is absent from `src_facts`, and lands in `added` → `FACT_SENTENCE_INVENTED_OR_MUTATED` → the **entire document reverts**. L7's cross-reference cannot ship at all without editing `DIGIT_RE`/`is_fact_sentence`. It is not merely illegal under CLASS I rule (1); it is a whole-document revert.

**The leak the fix opens.** To ship L7 you must exempt `Section \d+(\.\d+)?` from the digit test. That combines with a pre-existing widening at **line 455**:

```python
if any(s == a or a in s for a in approved):   # SUBSTRING containment, not equality
    continue
```

Register the cross-ref clause as an approved string (the natural implementation — it's a *clause*, not a sentence, so it can only pass check (b) via `a in s`), and now:

> `"Eloundou and co-authors show that exposure rises sharply with education, as discussed in Section 4."`

- digit masked by the new exemption → `is_fact_sentence` = **False** → escapes the fact-multiset check (a);
- non-fact → check (b) → contains the approved substring → **passes**;
- `validate_interpretation` is **never called on it** — check (b) is a pure membership test, not a re-validation.

A fabricated attribution (`Eloundou`, a new proper noun) plus a fabricated directional finding (`rises sharply`, a banned result verb) reaches the page with both deterministic backstops defeated by a two-line change. That is an ungrounded attribution on the page. The moat is breached.

**Second path (deletion is *not* safe here).** The contract asserts "deletion cannot fabricate." False in this pipeline. CLASS I rule (5) — ≥2 FACT sentences, ≥2 distinct sources, deictic pointer — and the fail-closed contradiction screen are validated **at injection time against the pre-L7 paragraph**. L7 runs *after* L2 freezes structure, i.e. after composition, on a banked artifact. When dedup deletes a premise fact or overflow-routing moves one out, the surviving class-I sentence ("Read together, these estimates suggest…") now floats above a *different* fact set, and its deictic points at whatever survived. `validate_reflow` never re-checks premise adjacency (line 455 is membership-only), so the contradiction screen is silently bypassed. Deletion cannot mint a number, but it **can un-ground an already-shipped relational claim and strip it of its screen**.

**Third:** the dedup criterion (`same [n]` + `>0.8 content-token overlap`) is lexical, not semantic. "1.8% have ≥10% of tasks exposed [12]" vs "46% have ≥10% of tasks exposed under LLM tooling [12]" share the same `[n]` and >0.8 of content tokens under `|A∩B|/min(|A|,|B|)`. Collapsing them deletes a distinct number, and the cross-ref then *asserts* that content is "discussed in Section X" where it is not — a false pointer.

**Fourth, independently fatal:** claimed effect is **+0.003 to +0.008**. SOTA_BRIEF_V2 §6 sets the smallest resolvable effect at **+0.0094 at 2σ (k=5)**. Even the fabrication-safe subset is unmeasurable on its own.

Relevant files: `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/reflow_report.py` (lines 232-234, 413-467, esp. 455), `/home/polaris/polaris_project/SOTA_BRIEF_V2.md` (§5 moat, §6 measurement).

input.reasoning:
REFUTED on the fabrication lens, with a concrete traced path.

HARD BLOCKER (verified in /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/reflow_report.py:232-234): `is_fact_sentence(s) = CITE_RE.search(s) or DIGIT_RE.search(s)` where DIGIT_RE = r"\d". Any digit-bearing sentence IS a FACT sentence, and validate_reflow (:438-448) enforces Counter(src_facts) == Counter(out_facts) byte-identically. L7's cross-reference clause "as discussed in Section 3.2" CONTAINS DIGITS. Any sentence carrying it is classified as an invented FACT sentence -> FACT_SENTENCE_INVENTED_OR_MUTATED -> the ENTIRE DOCUMENT REVERTS. So the cross-reference half of L7 cannot ship at all without editing is_fact_sentence/DIGIT_RE — the single routing function the whole moat rests on. It is not merely a CLASS I rule-(1) violation; it is a whole-document revert.

THE LEAK THE ONLY POSSIBLE FIX OPENS: to ship L7 you must exempt `Section \d+(\.\d+)?` from the digit test. That composes with a pre-existing widening at line 455: `if any(s == a or a in s for a in approved)` — SUBSTRING containment, not equality. The cross-ref is a *clause*, not a sentence, so the only way it passes check (b) is by registering it in `approved`, which turns a whole-sentence whitelist into a suffix whitelist. Concrete path:
  "Eloundou and co-authors show that exposure rises sharply with education, as discussed in Section 4."
  - digits masked by the new Section-exemption -> is_fact_sentence = False -> escapes the fact-multiset check (a);
  - non-fact -> check (b) -> contains approved substring -> PASSES;
  - validate_interpretation is NEVER invoked on it (check (b) is a pure membership test, not a re-validation).
Result: a fabricated ATTRIBUTION ("Eloundou", a new proper noun) + a fabricated directional finding ("rises sharply", a RESULT_VERBS_BANNED assertion) reaches the page with BOTH deterministic backstops defeated by a two-line change. That is an ungrounded attribution shipping. The moat is breached.

SECOND PATH — "deletion cannot fabricate" is FALSE in this pipeline. CLASS I rule (5) (>=2 facts, >=2 sources, deictic pointer, content-stem anchor) and the fail-closed contradiction screen are validated AT INJECTION TIME against the pre-L7 paragraph. L7 is specified to run AFTER L2 freezes structure, i.e. after composition, as a report->report transform. When dedup deletes a premise fact, or overflow-routing moves one out of a section, the surviving class-I sentence ("Read together, these estimates suggest...") now floats above a different fact set and its deictic points at whatever survived. validate_reflow never re-checks premise adjacency (line 455 is membership-only), so the contradiction screen is silently bypassed. Deletion cannot mint a number, but it CAN un-ground an already-shipped relational claim and strip it of the screen that licensed it. The drop-list exemption to the multiset check therefore does NOT preserve the guarantee it claims to.

THIRD — the dedup criterion is lexical, not semantic. "1.8% have >=10% of tasks exposed [12]" vs "46% have >=10% of tasks exposed under LLM tooling [12]" share the same [n] set and >0.8 content-token overlap under |A∩B|/min(|A|,|B|). Collapsing them DELETES a distinct number, and the surviving cross-ref then ASSERTS the deleted content is "discussed in Section X" when it is not — a false pointer (not a fabricated fact, but a false statement the validator cannot see).

FOURTH — independently fatal even for the safe subset: claimed effect +0.003 to +0.008 sits BELOW the smallest resolvable effect of +0.0094 at 2 sigma / k=5 (SOTA_BRIEF_V2 section 6). The lever is unmeasurable alone.

The lever's own admitted failure mode ("a misfiled fact is a coherence hit, not a faithfulness hit") is the weakest part of the analysis but is arguably defensible; the refutation does NOT rest on it. It rests on the digit-bearing cross-reference clause, which is mechanically incompatible with is_fact_sentence and whose only fix punches a hole in the exact function that routes every sentence in the document to its verification class.
input.fix:
SEVER AND HARDEN, or drop.

1. DELETE the cross-reference clause entirely. It is the sole fabrication carrier and it is severable. There is no digit-free, entity-free, non-vacuous form of it: a grammatical cross-ref needs a summarizing predicate, which is an unverified paraphrase of the deleted fact (not CLASS F), empirical (not CLASS I), and not a heading (not CLASS S). Do NOT touch DIGIT_RE or is_fact_sentence (reflow_report.py:232-234) under any circumstances — that function is the routing table for the whole contract.
   If a pointer is truly wanted, it must be CLASS S markdown chrome that strip_md removes from the prose stream (a heading or a table cell), never an in-prose clause — note strip_md (:241-250) only strips headings/table rows/captions, so an in-prose parenthetical is NOT class S.

2. FIX line 455 regardless of L7. `any(s == a or a in s for a in approved)` must become `any(s == a for a in approved)` (exact match). The substring rule already lets any digit-free sentence smuggle arbitrary prose around an approved fragment; L7 merely weaponizes it.

3. If the dedup+routing subset ships, implement the drop-list exemption as:
     out_facts - src_facts == {}          # additions remain STRICTLY forbidden
     src_facts - out_facts  ⊆ droplist    # deletions only from the audited list
   AND re-run validate_interpretation + the fail-closed contradiction screen on EVERY paragraph whose fact multiset changed (deleted, or gained/lost a routed fact), reverting that paragraph to its pre-L7 form on any failure. Without this, every deletion/reroute silently invalidates the premise-adjacency and contradiction guarantees of the class-I sentences left behind.

4. Change the dedup criterion from lexical overlap to "identical numeric multiset AND identical [n] set" — same-[n] + >0.8 token overlap demonstrably merges sentences carrying DIFFERENT numbers (1.8% vs 46%).

5. Do not test it alone: +0.003-0.008 is below the +0.0094 resolution floor. Bundle it into a larger arm or skip it.
